title: Vitamin D deficiency review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports clinician documentation of already reported vitamin D supplement use in qualified encounters without generating product, dose or duration.

source.title: Vitamin D: supplement use in specific population groups
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom public-health guidance; UAE thresholds, exposure and treatment pathways require adaptation
source.population: People at risk of low vitamin D status, with specific pregnancy, breastfeeding, age, skin pigmentation, sun-exposure and clinical-risk qualifiers.
source.setting: Public health and selected primary-care testing and supplement-use review.
source.exclusions: Exact for risk and qualified testing-context documentation only. It does not support automatic deficiency interpretation, dose, regimen, treatment duration or UAE laboratory thresholds.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1–6 and glossary — specific at-risk populations
exact_location.locator: official recommendations and linked definitions of specific population groups

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician recording of age, pregnancy or breastfeeding, low sun exposure, covering skin, darker skin and other entered risk context without diagnosing deficiency.

assessment.text: Supports documenting why an existing vitamin D test was clinically obtained and whether symptoms or high-risk context were assessed; it does not interpret the result or generate testing.
